description: The palatalization of dental stops often occurs before high front vowels or glides, leading to a shift from alveolar/dental to palatal articulation.
sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.

languages.description: Palatalization of /t/ to /c/ is a fundamental part of the slender/broad consonant contrast, triggered by a front vowel.
The /t/ assimilates with the following /j/ glide, resulting in a palatal stop /c/.
